heading: Chaplain Corps and Chief of Chaplains
label: 8082 Chaplain Corps and Chief of Chaplains

children.label: a
children.text: The Chaplain Corps is a staff corps of the Navy and shall be organized in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Navy.

children.label: b
children.text: There is in the executive part of the Department of the Navy the office of the Chief of Chaplains of the Navy. The Chief of Chaplains shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, from officers of the Chaplain Corps in the grade of commander or above who are serving on active duty and who have served on active duty in the Chaplain Corps for at least eight years.

children.label: c
children.text: An officer appointed as the Chief of Chaplains shall be appointed for a term of four years. However, the President may terminate or extend the appointment at any time.
The Chief of Chaplains shall perform such duties as may be prescribed by the Secretary of the Navy and by law.

The Chief of Chaplains shall, with respect to all duties pertaining to the procurement, distribution, and support of personnel of the Chaplain Corps, report to and be supported by the Chief of Naval Personnel.

children.label: e
children.text: The Chief of Chaplains, while so serving, holds the grade of rear admiral (upper half).
